fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The role of interchange in diagrammatic reasoning

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: In a 2-category, interchange is strict; in particular, this means that vertical composition and horizontal composition commute strictly with each other. This is very useful for string diagrams, but it is not strictly necessary. In a string diagram, strict interchange means that 2-cells can be freely moved upward and downward; so if you want to use diagrammatic reasoning to study something in a structure that has only weak interchange, you need two things:

fr:ol.fr:li: The vertical position of a 2-cell is fixed.
No two 2-cells should appear at the same vertical position.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: To move a 2-cell up or down is to insert an interchange cell. It is this principle that makes homotopy.io work.
